ground_truth.title: Full Unicode Search at 50× ICU Speed with AVX‑512
ground_truth.author: Ash Vardanian
ground_truth.main_content: This article is about the ugliest, but potentially most useful piece of open-source software I've written this year. It's messy, because UTF-8 is messy. The world's most widely used text encoding standard was introduced in 1989. It now covers more than 1 million characters across the majority of used writing systems, so it's not exactly trivial to work with. That's why ICU exists - pretty much the only comprehensive open-source library for Unicode and UTF-8 handling, powering Chrome/Chromium and probably every OS out there. It's feature-rich, battle-tested, and freaking slow. Now StringZilla makes some of the most common operations much faster, leveraging AVX-512 on Intel and AMD CPUs! Namely: Tokenizing text into lines or whitespace-separated tokens, handling 25 different whitespace characters and 9 newline variants; Case-folding text into lowercase form, handling all 1400+ rules and edge cases of Unicode 17 locale-agnostic expansions; Case-insensitive substring search bypassing case-folding for both European and Asian languages. StringZilla takes a different route: it tries very hard to find a fold-safe window in the needle - a slice that can be case-folded into ≤16 bytes, doesn't trigger surprises for a chosen SIMD path, and has enough byte diversity to be a good SIMD filter. The benchmark results show StringZilla achieving 20-150× faster performance than alternatives, or 20,000× faster when comparing to PCRE2 RegEx engine with case-insensitive flag.
with: Now StringZilla makes some of the most common operations much faster, leveraging AVX-512 on Intel and AMD CPUs!
StringZilla takes a different route: it tries very hard to find a fold-safe window in the needle - a slice that can be case-folded into ≤16 bytes.
Case-insensitive substring search bypassing case-folding for both European and Asian languages, available since v4.5.
The benchmark results show StringZilla achieving 20–150× faster performance than alternatives.
StringZilla is tested against a synthetic suite generated on the fly from the most recent Unicode specs.

items.alt: Searching UTF-8 in AVX-512: Safer and Faster
items.caption: Searching UTF-8 in AVX-512: Safer and Faster. The example contains multiple confusable characters: German Eszett variants 'ß' and 'ẞ', the Kelvin sign 'K' and ASCII 'k', and Greek mu 'μ' vs the micro sign 'µ'.
